behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: orchestrator/dispatcher must build and route three role-specific invocations correctly. This is more than a single local function check, but does not require real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment. Rejected System because behavior is about payload wiring, which in-process fakes can expose; rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model output content or multi-agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process fake reviewer clients for extractor/risk/compliance. Capture each invocation payload and assert: identical source document bytes/hash across all three calls, and each role gets its own instruction pack (no cross-role mix-up).

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly validates extraction quality from a live AI reviewer. A stub would hide failures in model interpretation/recall, especially with variation in phrasing. Rejected Workflow because this is one reviewer decision, not multiple agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real clause extractor on a curated contract set with known key terms. Assert required term categories are found with acceptable recall/precision thresholds; run multiple trials or tolerance bands to handle nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is infrastructure around the model call (payload/config propagation), not model judgment quality. Real AI output is not needed to detect wrong policy wiring. Rejected Agentic because behavior text disqualifies output-quality dependence; rejected System because in-process integration spies can catch config mismatch.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestrator with a fake risk-assessor adapter and a non-default policy profile. Assert invocation contains exact policy id/version/threshold fields and no fallback/default leakage.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model semantic judgment, especially indirect/euphemistic language detection. Deterministic mocks would mask this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this is a single reviewer’s model decision rather than multi-step agent pipeline behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real risk assessor against contracts/snippets containing direct and euphemistic risky language plus clean controls. Assert risky passages are flagged under the configured policy with expected sensitivity bounds across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module boundary (context/config handoff into compliance invocation). No real model output quality is required for this specific behavior. Rejected Agentic due explicit focus on parameter passing; rejected System since in-process adapter capture is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use orchestrator + fake compliance adapter with multiple jurisdiction/profile fixtures. Verify emitted invocation carries exact jurisdiction code and regulatory profile fields, including overrides and precedence rules.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Behavior tests correctness of AI-driven compliance determination, which depends on real model output quality and can vary across runs/prompts. Stubs cannot reveal misclassification against nuanced requirements. Rejected Workflow because only one model decision stage is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance checker on labeled contracts spanning compliant/non-compliant cases per jurisdiction profile. Assert decision accuracy and key requirement-level outcomes meet acceptance thresholds with repeat-run tolerance.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at cross-module data handoff/merge of heterogeneous reviewer outputs and attribution metadata, not just isolated algorithm logic. Integration fixtures from both reviewer output schemas are needed to catch mapping/normalization loss. Rejected System because no real OS/network is required; rejected Agentic because behavior validates deterministic merge semantics, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merge pipeline with realistic extractor and risk outputs for the same clause id/text but conflicting labels. Assert unified report contains both assessments, each attributed to its source reviewer, with no overwrite/drop.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a full pipeline with multiple agentic steps in sequence/parallel (three live reviewers) plus aggregation. Correctness depends on combined model outputs and orchestration across stages, so lower levels miss cross-step loss/coordination failures. Rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly requires end-to-end multi-step orchestration, not a single model call.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full production-like pipeline on real contracts using all three live reviewers. Capture each reviewer raw output and final merged report; assert every reviewer finding is represented (or traceably resolved) in the unified output with no dropped items.
